ddls:
CREATE TABLE "log_messages" (
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log message */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for related messages */,
  "time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the message was logged */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the message (e.g., Design Software, Fitter) */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the message (e.g., Info, Warning, Error) */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the message */,
  "flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating specific attributes of the message */,
  "suppressible" INTEGER /* Indicates if the message can be suppressed */,
  "file" TEXT /* File where the message originated */,
  "line" INTEGER /* Line number in the file where the message originated */,
  "location" TEXT /* JSON object containing additional location details */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the message is associated with */,
  "design_id" INTEGER /* ID of the design the message is associated with */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the message (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "context" TEXT /* Additional context or metadata about the message */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating if the issue has been resolved */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the issue was resolved */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to resolve the issue */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_messages_source FOREIGN KEY ("source") REFERENCES sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_messages_type FOREIGN KEY ("type") REFERENCES message_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_messages_file FOREIGN KEY ("file") REFERENCES files ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_messages_flag FOREIGN KEY ("flag") REFERENCES message_flags ("flag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "message_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the message type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the message type */,
  "color_code" TEXT /* Color code to visually distinguish the message type in the UI */,
  "icon" TEXT /* Icon to visually represent the message type in the UI */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "icon" TEXT /* Icon to visually represent the source in the UI */,
  "severity_threshold" TEXT /* Severity threshold that triggers alerts for messages from this source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file */,
  "path" TEXT /* Path to the file */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the file in bytes */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification to the file */,
  "checksum" TEXT /* Checksum to verify the integrity of the file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for quick contact */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the message */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log message being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the message was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the log message */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the log message */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique session ID for the user's session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES log_messages ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "message_flags" (
  "flag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each flag */,
  "flag_value" INTEGER /* The integer value of the flag */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the flag represents */,
  "color_code" TEXT /* Color code to visually distinguish the flag in the UI */,
  "icon" TEXT /* Icon to visually represent the flag in the UI */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flag_id")
)
